g.title: cluster_scenario2
g.text: Scenario 2: SSH Reconnect with Wrong Hostname

g.title: mapping
g.text: Routes never pass a SessionKind — the listener carries it.
Pool calls (StartForwarding/StopForwarding/...) are dispatched by listener.sessionKind.

g.title: invariants
g.text: forwardings and assignedAddrs are ALWAYS cleaned together
listener.route != nil does NOT guarantee forwarding exists
assignedAddrs is the ONLY source of truth for forwarding existence
Hostname validation happens in SSH Manager
(fail fast if server assigns wrong hostname)
GetAssignedAddresses uses RLock for concurrent reads
Lock hierarchy: clientMu acquired before addrNotifMu
listener.sessionKind is fixed at registration; routes never compute it.
Changing the Gateway annotation triggers stop-on-old / start-on-new.
isForwardingValid() detects stale state → clear listener.route → retry
Kubernetes exponential backoff handles retry timing

g.title: validation
g.text: For each addr in addrs: if contains(remoteHost) → return true
